evals.prompt: Review the current Orator redesign for FFTGenericChronicle. I am worried the skills are too weak, that Tame/Beast Tongue were removed even though monster recruitment and breeding are important, and that Brave/Faith policy was assumed instead of discussed. Produce a redesign review, not a final implementation.
evals.expected_output: A review that diagnoses Orator against combat-skill value, campaign/roster system preservation, monster recruitment/breeding access, Brave/Faith as a dependent global system, long-term policy, and identifies the package as needing revision rather than defending weak numbers.

evals.prompt: Redesign Thief's Steal, Poach, Treasure Hunter, and Steal Heart package. Keep in mind that not every action has to be useful in a single battle, but every skill must justify its value somewhere in the game. Also check whether Steal Heart belongs thematically to Thief or another job.
evals.expected_output: A proposal that separates combat-facing steals from campaign/economy/monster-facing skills, preserves Poach or gives an explicit access promise, uses per-skill value cards, and performs thematic ownership review rather than blindly preserving vanilla placement.

evals.prompt: The Knight and Archer proposals had cool mechanics but too many marks/statuses for a player to track. Create a revision process that keeps the good mechanics while simplifying visibility.
evals.expected_output: A process or review that lists mechanics worth preserving, identifies opacity/complexity risks, requires visible representation for every mark/status/zone, and suggests simpler visible expressions instead of deleting the mechanics.

evals.prompt: Review an Orator proposal where Entice charms an enemy temporarily during battle but still permanently recruits them after battle. Also review Fast Talk having a once-per-round clause. Do not rewrite the whole job; focus on process failures.
evals.expected_output: A review that flags battle/campaign incoherence for Entice, questions invisible or unintuitive per-round limitations, and recommends resolving the story/mechanics relationship before accepting the skill card.

evals.prompt: Create a first-pass redesign direction for Orator without being constrained to the vanilla skill list. Preserve FFT identity, recruitment, monster access, social manipulation, guns, and Brave/Faith if the system remains useful.
evals.expected_output: A direction that separates identity pillars from vanilla names, generates ambitious alternatives, preserves protected systems, and explains which vanilla skills could be kept, moved, replaced, or removed.
